calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-20-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Peck, via ZOOM, for Manuel Martir, Jr., in person, Petitioner; Thomas LaLanne, via ZOOM, for Maxwell Martir, Patricia Martir, and Julio Martir, Objectors.

The court states that it is ready to take the matter under submission and asks if counsel has anything to add for the court's consideration. None are presented. Mr. Peck notes that he submitted a revised [proposed] order and asks for confirmation that the court has the order. The court advises Mr. Peck to confirm the preceding with the appointed Examiner.

The Accounting Petition is submitted.

Verified Petition For Order Directing Personal Representative Manuel Martir, Jr. To Sell Estate Property
Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas LaLanne, in court, for Yessica Martir, Julio Martir, Max, and Patricia Martir, via ZOOM, Objectors; Ronald Peck, via ZOOM, for Manuel Martir, Jr., in court, Personal Representative.

Mr. Peck reports that his clients' intent was not to sell the real property he lives in but that the plan was for his client to buy-out his half-siblings and that in fact, his client has been paying the mortgage, property taxes, and home insurance for some time and wants to close this probate and file a partition action. Mr. LaLanne confirms that the beneficiaries want the home to be sold and at one point believed that the proceeds would be distributed in-kind. Mr. Peck and Mr. LaLanne offer additional testimony to support their positions. The court points counsel to probate code 9613.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Order Directing Personal Representative, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Continued To Jul-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Peck appeared by zoom for and with Manuel Martir (Petitioner) in Court.
Thomas LaLanne appeared by zoom for and with Yessica Martir by zoom and Julio Martir by zoom (Beneficiaries), and for Maxwell Martir and Patricia Martir (Beneficiaries).

The Court noted that the March 11th response never made it to the Court's file, questions why Mr. Peck didn't follow up with the electronic filing, and stated that it is up to Mr. Peck to follow up and make sure it made it to the file. Mr. Peck stated that the stipulation to have the matter sent to pro bono mediation didn't make it to the file as well, that he agrees that he should have checked, and spoke of what happened at the March 30th hearing and his inability to connect via zoom even though he was on zoom. Mr. LaLanne stated that the order to appear was issued to Mr. Peck and that he was not at March 30th hearing because his Client had a deposition that spilled over into the next day. Mr. Peck stated that they finished mediation, but they did not settle, and requested a 45 to 60-day compliance hearing date for filing of a petition for final distribution. The Court noted that this has been on for calendar for months for status on close and that it requires filing of petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of a report on status of administration. Mr. Peck stated that he wouldn't be filing a petition for final distribution with the amount of litigation in this case, that the parties saw someone from the Pro Bono Panel and that they will talk to see if further mediation will be helpful. Manual Martir stated that he still does not know what's going on, that it seems like this is taking forever and that he would like this to be resolved as soon as possible. The order to show cause re sanctions and all of the orders to appear are discharged and off calendar. Status on close of estate is continued to July 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Peck is to either file for final distribution, or for approval of a status report by June 30, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition By Administrator Manuel Martir, Jr. To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property And For Order Authorizing And Directing The Transfer Of Estate Property To Claimant Manuel Martir, Jr., Individually
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Continued To Dec-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Demurrer To First Amended Petition Of Administrator Manuel Martir, Jr. To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property And For Order Authorizing And Directing The Transfer Of Estate Property To Claimant Manuel Martir, Jr., Individually
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas J. Lalanne, Esq. for for Patricia Martir, Yessica Martir, Julio Martir and Maxwell Martir.
Ronald Peck, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for and with Manuel Martir (who was present in court).

Matter called. Arugment on the Demurrer To First Amended Petition Of Administrator Manuel Martir, Jr. To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property And For Order Authorizing And Directing The Transfer Of Estate Property To Claimant Manuel Martir, Jr., Individually is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adoted the tentative ruling as follows: the demurrer to the First Amended Petition is sustained without leave to amend. A long line of cases beginning with Norton v. Bassett (1908) 154 Cal. 411, 415-16) and continuing through Martin v. Kehl (1983) 145 Cal.App. 3d 228, 240 establishes that a voluntary resulting trust becomes an involuntary constructive trust by operation of law upon the voluntary trustee's death. These cases hold that an involuntary constructive trust, created by operation of law following the death of a voluntary trustee, is inherently adverse to the beneficiary's interests and automatically begins the statutory limitations period running at the voluntary trustee's death without the requirement of the constructive trustee's repudiation of the trust. Estate of Yool (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 867 is not to the contrary. The issue on appeal in Yool was whether the one-year statute of limitations under CCP section 366.2 applied to the petitioner's constructive trust claim. Yool's analysis turned on whether a personal claim existed against the decedent voluntary trustee as of the time of death. Such a claim could have been triggered if the decedent had repudiated his trust obligations. Had such a claim existed, there would have been a pre-death personal claim against decedent that, in turn, could have triggered the section 366.2 limitation period. "[A]t the time of Yool's death [petitioner] had suffered no harm." Id. At 877. Yool's discussion of repudiation is pre-death, emphasizing that the issue of who owned the property "only arose after Yool's death." Id. Therefore, since there was no pre-death claim arising from the pre-death resulting trust, section 366.2 was not triggered. The court did not consider or address whether the four-year statute of limitations in section 343 applied or began to run, nor would it have had occasion to consider that issue since the claim in that case was brought within four years of the voluntary trustee's death. Because the petition in this case was brought well beyond the four-year limitations period, the petition is barred by section 343. In reaching this conclusion, the Court does not consider any other claims Petitioner may have with respect to the property or his investments in it. The Court stated that that form of order should be left with the clerk. The Court also stated that to the extent that Mr. Peck wants to raise any objections to the form of order that it should be done in an expedited fashion.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Peck for and with Manuel Martir, Jr.
Thomas Lalanne for Patricia Martir, Yessica Martir, Julio Martir and Maxwell Martir.

Mr. Peck stated that they have exchanged messages, that they have not spoken of a neutral administrator, that the only asset of the estate involves his Client's property and that title is the real issue. Mr. Peck also stated that there is no money to pay a neutral, that his Client has lived in the house for fifteen years, and that his Client has paid the mortgage and has made improvements to the property. Furthermore, Mr. Peck stated that his Client is the oldest Son, that his Client has a significant greater interest in the property, that the Father appointed the Son as Attorney in Fact for the purpose of helping his Son qualify for the loan and that this came about because his Client is in the process of refinancing at a lower interest rate. Mr. Lalanne stated that this is hearsay, that Manuel never paid any rent, that there is a conflict with having him appointed, suggested appointment of joint administrators or a neutral, stated that he does not know where the money would come from to pay a neutral and that he would have to speak with his Clients. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration are taken under submission. Mr. Lalanne is to provide a proposed order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
